sutta: once when the melba ananda was dwelling near kosambi in gosita the very visited him and after exchanging the usual polite talk sat down at 1 side and so seated he said to the verbal ananda this robin sir was said by the deva panchala chanda from news escape he found quick sage who walked to jhana awaken seer a depth withdrawn now what sir is the news and what the the way of escape declared by the exalted and november ananda replied these 5 strands of sense desire revenge are called the nose by the exalted 1 forms cognized by the eye long for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire sounds recognized by the institutes by the tongue contacts by the touch long for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire these 5 strands of sense desire are called the nose by the exalted 1 consider a reverend sir the monk who aloud from sen's desire enters and abides in the first jhana thus far reverend sir in 1 particular did the exalted 1 declare a way of escape from the nose and there too is the nose and what is there insofar as thought applied and sustained is not ended that there is the nose now consider sir the monk who enters and abides in the second jhana the third the fourth in the sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness of nothingness of neither perception or non-perception the ending of perception and feeling and ceased by wisdom that the asavas are completely destroyed thus far and with no further particular did the exalted 1 did clear a way of escape from the nose that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the monk is asking the rebel ananda what is the nose the nose is like the rope that is tied around 1's neck when you're going to hang somebody here you put a nose around his neck this rope around his neck so [Music] the 5 strands of sense desire forms sounds smells taste and touch these are the these are the things that bind us like a nose on around our neck we are caught by mara mara has put a rope around our neck and we are in control of mara because of the 5 senses and the way of escapement that the buddha has declared now is the first jhana the second jana third fought jhana sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness nothingness neither perception or non-perception and in the ending of perception and feeling these are the ways of escapement from mara's nose and and this is another suta that shows how important the janas are if you want to escape from the nose that mara has put around our neck so definitely the janas are the teachings of the buddhas and sutas the buddha has declared that the way of escape or from liberation from samsara and from mara is by attaining the janas because from the jaanas when we attain the janus the 5 hindrances are abandoned and when the 5 indians are abandoned then we see things clearly as they really are and from there we get inside wisdom then next is
